text:
Market today: Risk-on day driven by Tesla's self-driving update lifting EV/tech sentiment and SpaceX news flow moving space names. Broader tape is firm but your margin book is still carrying two losers that need a decision.

Your holdings: AVGO (your largest, -14.66%) is flagged TRIM — you're underwater and on margin, a further leg down hits hard; the stop at 336 is your line but don't wait for it to act. PLTR (-11.08%, SELL) — you're losing money on a name already flagged for exit; no reason to hold. SPCX is a brand-new, very volatile IPO with limited price history — on margin this can gap through your stop fast; size accordingly.

Watch: 1) AVGO — any broad chip/semi weakness could accelerate your loss; watch SMH as your canary. 2) SpaceX news (Rocket Lab acquisition, Charter deal) could whipsaw SPCX and SPAL hard in either direction.

Bottom line: Stop sitting on PLTR and AVGO losses — margin means those aren't just paper cuts.
